issue_title: Refresh the search index more frequently
issue_body:
We want to refresh the search index more frequently. I'm going to look into the primary bottlenecks and see how I can enable more frequent refreshing without making it an over-kill for the search nodes. 

Currently, we're aiming for a 3-6 hour refresh time. I'll update the issue once I know more.

I've got several core nodes running and I get very slow performance from RPC requests. When calling the `getinfo` request, I'm getting response times between 30s and 60s. Very occasionally, I get an immediate response, but I haven't been able to replicate it enough to determine circumstances to reliably produce it. I've tried everything below multiple times on fresh infrastructure and have gotten the same results each time, with one exception (see **Curiosities** below).

**The "Metal"**
One core node process running in a docker image that's running on a m4.large EC2 instance. The base operating system on the EC2 instance is the standard Amazon Linux AMI.

**The Setup**
On the base AMI:
- Install docker and start the service:
```
yum -y install docker
service docker start
```
- create directory structure for data:
```
mkdir -p /data/{server,api}
```
- Write basic configuration for the blockstack server:
```
printf "[bitcoind]\np2p_port = 8333\nregtest = False\nspv_path = /root/.virtualchain-spv-headers.dat\nserver = bitcoin.blockstack.com\npasswd = blockstacksystem\nuser = blockstack\ntimeout = 300\nport = 8332\nuse_https = False\n[blockstack]\nemail = foo@bar.com\nserver_version = 0.17.07\n" > /data/server/blockstack-server.ini
```
- Bootstrap the core node with `fast_sync`
```
docker run -a stdout \
    -v /data/server/:/root/.blockstack-server/ \
    --name blockstack-core-init \
    quay.io/blockstack/blockstack-core:v0.17.0.14 \
    blockstack-core --debug fast_sync http://fast-sync.blockstack.org/snapshot.bsk > /dev/null
```
- When the fast_sync has completed, start the blockstack server, exposing the port out to the instance:
```
docker run -d \
    -v /data/server/:/root/.blockstack-server/ \
    -v /data/api/:/root/.blockstack/ \
    -p 0.0.0.0:6264:6264 \
    quay.io/blockstack/blockstack-core:v0.17.0.14 \
    blockstack-core start --debug --foreground
```
- Then, wait for the core node to catch up the rest of the way

**One slow call**
```
curl -X POST \
  http://my.ec2.hostname.or.ip:6264/RPC2 \
  -H 'content-length: 95' \
  -H 'content-type: application/xml' \
  -d '<?xml version="1.0"?><methodCall><methodName>getinfo</methodName><params></params></methodCall>'
```
**Resource consumption during the above call**
`top` reports half of the available memory free
`top` reports very little CPU usage. I saw it "spike" to 1.38% for one refresh a three times.
`iotop` reports disk activity to be basically nothing

The layered filesystem of docker shouldn't play into this, because I'm mounting all the data in as a volume.

**Curiosities**
Using identical steps (via a user-data script attached to a launch configuration) on a t2.small, I was able to get great response times for hundreds of costlier requests (crawling through zonefile history of the `.id` namespace) for a short amount of time.

**Some possibilities**
I dug around to try to find external causes for sluggishness, since it doesn't appear the core process is doing any work during the 30-60 seconds it takes to get a response.

- Slow Performance by `bitcoin.blockstack.org`. The core node is configured to use this as a bitcoind node and bitcoind is used in the `getinfo` call: https://github.com/blockstack/blockstack-core/blob/master/blockstack/blockstackd.py#L827
- Contention on the local file that contains info about the last block? https://github.com/blockstack/blockstack-core/blob/master/blockstack/lib/nameset/virtualchain_hooks.py#L181

What do you all think?

issue_title: Set an environment variable with current test
issue_body:
When executing tests in CI, sometimes a test may hang indefinitely. One can look at the output using `-v` but that might not always be reliable.

If each worker would set a specific environment variable with the test it is executing, one can use a process monitoring library like `psutil` to figure out which worker is running which test. This could be a variable named something like `PYTEST_XDIST_CURRENT_TEST` which is set to the test node id. Alternatively, we could also add if the test is in the `setup`, `call` or `teardown` phase.

The implementation should be trivial and can be really helpful when diagnosing CI problems.

@RonnyPfannschmidt what do you think?

issue_title: Add NIC to devices under test
issue_body:
Request #865 requires that we connect to another interface on BIG-IP other than the management interface in order to successfully run functional tests.

We need to add a NIC and pre-establish:

- a vlan connected to the test host
- a self-ip with default port-lockdown settings (unless DUT port is out of range, then that will need to be specified in port-lockdown settings) and in a network attached to and reachable from the test host

From jenkins output:

test/functional/auth/test_user.py:100: AssertionError
https://travis-ci.org/F5Networks/f5-icontrol-rest-python/builds/103147498

issue_title: Create new partner form has no class or method
issue_body:
Using the dashboard to add a new partner does not work, as the form has no method.

    <form class="form-NULL NULL" method="NULL">

hi,
I create a the following offer according to the doc. but I find it doesn't work as expected.
_offer: Buy 1 Lonely Planet books, get £5 off a Lonely Planet DVD
1. Create a range for Lonely Planet books and another for Lonely Planet DVDs
2. Use a count-based condition linking to the book range with a value of '**1**'
3. Use a fixed discount benefit that links to the DVD range and has a value of 5._
This offer only applies when I add one more product outside the Lonely Planet books range. 
it can't work even if I just add 2 products in Lonely Planet books range.
the workaround is to change the value in step 2) to '0' instead of '1', then everything goes ok.

issue_title: .NET Core SDK for Windows Installation Path Problem
issue_body:
The .NET Core SDK for Windows installer
[https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=809122](url)
has an option to set installation path other than C:\Program Files\dotnet.
Unfortunately, the installer always installs it to C:\Program Files\dotnet even if you change the installation path option.
Please fix it if it is a bug.

issue_title: Request Headers are being Cached/Saved on HttpContext from Previous Requests.
issue_body:
# Issue Title

When Accessing the HttpContext.Request.Headers values are cached/saved/magic appear in different and independent requests.

# General
ASP.NET Core 2

I am sending the Bearer token (JWT) to authenticate the requests from the FE to the API.
Some requests did not send a token as they required no authentication. 
So the code was like this.

`  if (!String.IsNullOrEmpty(token))
   {
     client.DefaultRequestHeaders.Authorization = new AuthenticationHeaderValue("Bearer", token);
    }`

When a route needed to get info about the User it checked the JWT 

`context.Request.Headers.TryGetValue("Authorization", out var accessToken);
`
if I did not send a token and a **previous** request from different browser send a token, then that token was used.

# My Solution
I now always send a Request Header Authorization.

# Why does this happen? Here is the API startup file
https://gist.github.com/drakoumel/2a36f10607170cbb92e36caa286c315f

issue_title: System.Threading.Overlapped fails to install in a Xamarin project
issue_body:
# NuGet package `System.Threading.Overlapped` fails to install in a Xamarin project

## General

Xamarin projects are still using `packages.config` and the _old_ NuGet system, so no `PackageReference` in the `.csproj`. I think that is why it is still looking for a `lib` folder with .NET Standard assemblies and why it is interpreting the NuGet package incorrectly.

I experienced this with an Android app, however looks like it doesn't really matter which kind of Xamarin project you're using as described in https://github.com/xamarin/xamarin-macios/issues/3138

I manually added the package using a `PackageReference` in my csproj, which succeeded. But then the app crashes at runtime when trying to load the assemblies.

The other workaround to avoid the Win32 files mentioned in [the issue](https://github.com/xamarin/xamarin-macios/issues/3138) didn't work for me and failed with the following compilation error:

```
/Library/Frameworks/Mono.framework/Versions/5.4.1/lib/mono/xbuild/Microsoft/NuGet/Microsoft.NuGet.targets(5,5): Error: Your project.json doesn't have a runtimes section. You should add '"runtimes": { "unix": { } }' to your project.json and then re-run NuGet restore.
```

Just create any Xamarin project (Android app, iOS app...) and try to install `System.Threading.Overlapped` with Visual Studio for Mac.

issue_title: Set upgrade-insecure-requests where we can do so safely
issue_body: This will be a significant usability win for users who are facing mixed content situations.

issue_title: Don't use the default UA when verifying challenges
issue_body: While related to #858, I feel like this is a slightly different issue. We have had reports of people running into problems with the User-Agent when using the `manual` plugin. `manual` uses `acme` to validate that the challenge has been set up correctly before responding to the server. This is done using the `requests` library whose default UA as of writing this e-mail is `python-requests/2.8.1`. Some webservers are rejecting requests with this UA, causing self validation to fail, and `letsencrypt` to exit.

issue_title: /etc/letsencrypt/configs should possibly be called something else
issue_body:
... because its name may confuse people (they would expect it to have configuration in general, not specifically renewal configuration). But what name would be clearest?

Cc @pde.
Does let's encrypt have any support for java web servers? If it's just apache and nginx, that'd be pretty limited.
Once we switch `*-auto` to installing `certbot` packages, we should have it continue to install `letsencrypt` the script still works for people reaching into the venv.

When I go through the notes for [running local copy of client](https://letsencrypt.readthedocs.org/en/latest/contributing.html#running-a-local-copy-of-the-client). When I get to the step where I run `letsencrypt`, I get

```
(venv)➜  letsencrypt git:(master) ✗ letsencrypt
An unexpected error occurred:
IOError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: '/var/log/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log'
Please see the logfile 'letsencrypt.log' for more details.
```
The log file says
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/blake/github/letsencrypt/venv/bin/letsencrypt", line 9, in <module>
    load_entry_point('letsencrypt', 'console_scripts', 'letsencrypt')()
  File "/home/blake/github/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/cli.py", line 1366, in main
    setup_logging(args, _cli_log_handler, logfile='letsencrypt.log')
  File "/home/blake/github/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/cli.py", line 1279, in setup_logging
    args, logfile=logfile, fmt=fmt)
  File "/home/blake/github/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/cli.py", line 1250, in setup_log_file_handler
    log_file_path, maxBytes=2 ** 20, backupCount=10)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/logging/handlers.py", line 117, in __init__
    BaseRotatingHandler.__init__(self, filename, mode, encoding, delay)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/logging/handlers.py", line 64, in __init__
    logging.FileHandler.__init__(self, filename, mode, encoding, delay)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/logging/__init__.py", line 905, in __init__
    StreamHandler.__init__(self, self._open())
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/logging/__init__.py", line 935, in _open
    stream = open(self.baseFilename, self.mode)
IOError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: '/var/log/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log'
```

I is this log file supposed to be created during the setup and installation? I could not find where this is supposed to happen.

Or am I supposed to set this up myself, and this is just not mentioned in the documentation? If so I can add that.

```
$ letsencrypt --version
usage:
  letsencrypt [SUBCOMMAND] [options] [domains]

The Let's Encrypt agent can obtain and install HTTPS/TLS/SSL certificates.  By
default, it will attempt to use a webserver both for obtaining and installing
the cert.
letsencrypt: error: unrecognized arguments: --version
```

Verified in 523dba0b999ec46daf8c74edbfe1c29a3b43d8d2

issue_title: Unclosed consumer causes application to hang on exception
issue_body:
I've been able to reproduce this error with minimal code:

1. Create a `Consumer` configured with `group.id` set to `None`
2. Raise an exception (outside any try/except)

One would expect the stack trace to print and the application to exit; however, after the stack trace prints, the application hangs indefinitely. Calling `close` on the `Consumer` instance before raising the exception results in the correct behavior.

Just upgraded a project from Django 1.7 to 1.8 and installed 9cc56318b. When I attempted to create a new migration, I got the following error:

    django.core.exceptions.ImproperlyConfigured: SalesforceAutoField must be an auto created primary key with name 'id' (as configured by settings).

This error was not clear to me and didn't suggest any specific fix since as far as I know, the pk's *are* named 'id'. The only way I was able to resolve this error was to delete the existing initial migration and recreate it. The only significant change I could detect was going from:

    ('id', salesforce.fields.SalesforceAutoField(default=None, primary_key=True, db_column='Id', serialize=False)),

to:

    ('id', salesforce.fields.SalesforceAutoField(auto_created=True, verbose_name='ID', db_column='Id', primary_key=True, serialize=False)),

Once I edited the existing migration to change this line for each model (all created via inspectdb), the problem went away.

Was there some setting I was supposed to change along the way? Or was it expected that migrations created in 1.7 would need to be edited in place for 1.8? Either way, some note in the README would be helpful and appreciated. Thank you @philchristensen and @hynekcer for your work on this library, it has been very useful!

issue_title: Decimal objects should respect as_string argument at either the validation or deserialization stages
issue_body:
The `Decimal` object subclasses `Number` which establishes an `as_string` constructor parameter in order to serialize values as string.

While this is a good idea and probably caters to a canonical use-case in serialization, there is the potential for unexpected behaviour in deserialization and/or validation (when using the `Decimal` object, specifically).

This is because the Python `decimal.Decimal()` method constructs values from integers, strings, floats, or tuples; and (per https://docs.python.org/2.7/library/decimal.html#quick-start-tutorial):

> Construction from an integer or a float performs an exact conversion of the value of that integer or float

From the Python documentation, even with `as_string` set, the current behaviour is to deserialize by passing directly to `decimal.Decimal()`, causing a situation like this:

```
>>> Decimal('3.14')
Decimal('3.14')
>>> Decimal(3.14)
Decimal('3.140000000000000124344978758017532527446746826171875')
```

This behaviour is at best arcane but at worst unexpected or wrong regardless of whether `as_string` is set.

I propose that when `as_string` _is_ set to true, either the `_deserialize` method implicitly casts the value to a string before passing to `decimal.Decimal()` or non-string values do not pass validation.

I prefer the implicit coercion to a string and can submit a small pull request to implement this. Failing validation is a bit more difficult because you have to differentiate between `str` and `unicode` - but I have presented it here as a potential solution to the problematic behaviour for comparison.

issue_title: Learning to rank using explicit preferences / rankings?
issue_body:
Hi @maciejkula , thank you for your fantastic library and examples as I have found them really helping in learning about implicit recommender systems. I just have a few questions that you can hopefully weigh in on.

I am an undergraduate CS student currently working on a project which involves eliciting user preferences using a game with purpose. (quite similar to the game described in this [paper by Hacker and Von Ahn](https://dl.acm.org/citation.cfm?id=1518882))
In short users are paired up and asked to indicate which of two movies (gathered from the MovieLens dataset) they would prefer while their partner is tasked with predicting their decision, and both players take turns expressing preferences and making predictions.
I have started to look at implementing a recommender system (based on the RelativeSVD algorithm described in the paper above) which works on data gathered from the game and have built one in python which performs gradient descent using (i,j,k) triplets indicating user i preferred item i to item j in the game and therefore trying to ensure item j is rated higher than item k in the (num_users x num_items) predicted score matrix.

I came across LightFM and noticed some similarities in this methodology and the implicit recommender built into LightFM which makes use of BPR and WARP loss, and would like to build and evaluate a LightFM model to work on the game data for comparison.
1. Would it be possible to adapt the LightFM model to work on explicit (positive_item, negative_item) pairings? I assume this would require changes to the underlying Cython responsible for implementing the BPR and WARP loss functions which may be non trivial. I also came across Spotlight when researching this which may offer a [slightly more friendly way of customizing the sampling](https://github.com/maciejkula/spotlight/blob/master/spotlight/factorization/implicit.py#L258) if I wanted to attempt to implement this myself?

Another idea I'm experimenting with is having users build up personal rankings of movies and having a reccomender work on these. For example imagine we have 10 movies with ids 0-9 and the following partial ordering for a user: `[1,4,5,6,8]` . Movie 1 has 4 movies ranked below it so it gets a value of 4, movie 4 has 3 below so it's value is 3 etc. down to movie 8 with a score of 0 (along with all other unordered movies)
Ideally I would like movies with higher values to appear higher up in the predicted scores and I'm using a `ndcg@k` evaulation to check this (with relevance being a scaled version of each movies interaction value)

2. Will LightFM work well with these sort of values in the interaction matrix? I notice the [linked example which works on sketchfab models](http://blog.ethanrosenthal.com/2016/11/07/implicit-mf-part-2/) treats interactions as likes which would be somewhat similar however a lot of the current evaluation seems to be focused on positive examples being non-zero and negative examples being zero (e.g binary interactions), but for this data it's important that a higher positive value is placed higher above smaller positive values. Based on issue #260 I believed that using `sample_weight` would improve things and yet from what I can see it makes no discernible affect on the outcome of training the model. Curiously, there seems to be no difference when using these positive interaction values which decrease alongside rank versus binary interactions with 1s indicating which items appear in the users partial ranking. This makes it difficult to optimize for `ndcg@k`. I'm wondering if perhaps I'm using the `sample_weight` argument incorrectly here? For the small example above movies `[1,4,5,6,8]` would have an interaction value of 1 and weights of `[4,3,2,1,0]` respectively. The idea being that ideally we would like to preserve as much of the original partial ranking in our predictions while generating a total ranking with which to make recommendations.

Apologies for the long post, any advice or suggestions with how to handle this data using LightFM (or another library if more suitable for the task) would be appreciated. If anything is unclear feel free to ask and I'll be happy to clarify. Thanks!

It looks like this is related to the downloads from the NREL website. I'm not sure what the limitations are on that.

issue_title: Create an example being used with mypy/python3.6 enhancements for ClassVar
issue_body:
Per the mypy documentation:
http://mypy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/python36.html#syntax-for-variable-annotations-pep-526

I think it has to be done like this:

```python
import dependency_injector.containers as containers
import dependency_injector.providers as providers
from my_services import MyService
from my_drivers import MyDriver


class Services(containers.DeclarativeContainer):
    my_service = providers.Singleton(MyService, driver=MyDriver) # type: ClassVar[MyService]
```

Though I **think** this might also work:


```
class Services(containers.DeclarativeContainer):
    my_service: ClassVar[MyService] = providers.Singleton(MyService, driver=MyDriver)
```

I don't get completions in Visual Studio Code for the latter, so I don't know if that's actually the way to do it. The mypy docs don't show assignments here: http://mypy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/python36.html#syntax-for-variable-annotations-pep-526 unless it's done with the `# type: ` syntax.

Z3 computes a wrong interpolant for the example below.

I think this is due to the "rounding off" done in order to deal with
integer inequalities, which is performed regardless of whether the input
inequality is over the integer or over the rationals.

I'm also attaching a tentative patch for the issue.

Here's an SMT-LIB2 example showing the problem:
-----------------------------------------------

(set-logic QF_LRA)

(declare-fun x () Real)
(declare-fun y () Real)
(declare-fun z () Real)

(define-fun A () Bool
  (and
   (not (<= x 0.0))
   (>= (+ (* 10.0 y) (* (- 9.0) x)) 0.0)
   )
  )

(define-fun B () Bool
  (and (>= z 0.0)
       (<= (+ (* 10.0 y) (* 9.0 z)) 0.0))
  )

(compute-interpolant (and (interp A) B))
(exit)

;; computed wrong interpolant:
(define-fun I () Bool
  (>= y (/ 9.0 10.0)))
;; a correct interpolant is: (not (<= y 0.0))

(assert (or (and A (not I)) (and B I)))
(check-sat)


Here's a tentative patch:
-------------------------

diff --git a/src/interp/iz3proof_itp.cpp b/src/interp/iz3proof_itp.cpp
--- a/src/interp/iz3proof_itp.cpp
+++ b/src/interp/iz3proof_itp.cpp
@@ -2590,6 +2590,9 @@
         if(pstrict && qstrict && round_off)
             Qrhs = make(Sub,Qrhs,make_int(rational(1)));
 #else
+        if (round_off && get_type(Qrhs) != int_type()) {
+            round_off = false;
+        }
         bool pstrict = op(P) == Lt;
         if(qstrict && round_off && (pstrict || !(c == make_int(rational(1))))){
             Qrhs = make(Sub,Qrhs,make_int(rational(1)));

issue_body:
Tested on HEAD: 082936b

The following input takes very long for z3 to solve. When "(mod x x)" is changed to "0" z3 solves this instantly. The same when  "(* (mod x x) x)" is changed to "(mod x x)".

```
(set-logic NIA)
(define-fun f ((x Int)) Int (mod x (* (mod x x) x)))
(declare-fun x () Int)
(assert (not (and (= (f x) (f (+ x 10)))
(= (f 1) 3)
(= (f 2) 6)
(= (f 3) 9)
(= (f 4) 2)
(= (f 5) 5)

```

First of all, I'd like to take the chance to thank you for Lasagne. I tried several different frameworks built on top of Theano and none is as easy to debug, quick to extend and simple to use.

That said, there are a few things that might be improved and I would like to begin a discussion on some design choices. 

*Disclaimer: the following points are to be intended "at the best of my knowledge", i.e., I might state something wrong, or have missed an existing way to get what I want and/or some major disadvantages of my proposals.*

1. Lasagne is *mostly* an object-oriented framework. Would it be possible to go fully object-oriented? The immediate advantage I see is twofold: a) layers that do something not-ordinary could override the default functions to define their "extra-ordinary behavior" (e.g., I recently implemented a layer with sublayers and I found a way to hack Lasagne by setting `self.input_layer` in a convenient way, but that's a sketchy solution and is difficult to read and understand), b) one would not need to always import `get_output` and `get_output_shape` when debugging in ipdb, as they would be class methods.
[This old code] (https://github.com/fvisin/arctic_layers/blob/79cc6c5d5f3eab399980f5fa11e468e25641323e/layers.py#L126) that I wrote before switching to Lasagne does something in this fashion (see e.g. the `get_all_params` method).
2. I couldn't find a simple and efficient way to do symbolical shape inference. Say I have multiple convolutions/pooling layers and I want to know for each of them if there has been some implicit cropping at run-time. The only way I found is to call `get_output(theLayer).shape`, but (please correct me if I am wrong!) it traverses the full graph of layers every time, which is time consuming. It would be nice to store the partial information in the Layer so that there is no need to recompute it every time. As a plus this would also allow an easy access to the `tag.test_value` of the output symbolic variable of the Layer, e.g. with `theLayer.output.tag.test_value` making debug much easier.
3. The `get_all_params` helper function provides a convenient way to access all the params of the Layers of the network, filtering them by `tag`. It would be nice to add a way to filter by the name of the layer (e.g., with regexp). For similar reasons, it would be nice if the Layers of the library allowed to add custom tags to the parameters. For example [conv.py](https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/blob/0440814d4e7936de8423c29bbf9d5423ccc28ee8/lasagne/layers/conv.py#L234) currently sets `regularizable=False` on the bias, but could also set other tags from a dictionary of tags provided to the constructor. One way to do it could be to add a `tags` parameter to the Layer, which would be a dictionary with as keys the names of the parameters for which we want to set a tag and as value a dictionary of tags and relative values.

Let me know if any of these sounds reasonable.

If I pass a project dict to a progen project, with the paths already set to be relative to the export directory, there are issues. 

This function:
```python
def _generate_output_dir(settings, path):
        """ This is a separate function, so that it can be more easily tested """
        relpath = os.path.relpath(settings.root,path)
        count = relpath.count(os.sep) + 1

        return relpath+os.path.sep, count
```
used in this context:
````python
self.project['export']['output_dir']['rel_path'], self.project['export']['output_dir']['rel_count'] = self._generate_output_dir(self.settings, path)
```
Where rel_path is FINALLY used:
```python
 fix_paths(self.project['export'], self.project['export']['output_dir']['rel_path'], list(FILES_EXTENSIONS.keys()) + ['include_paths', 'source_paths'])
```
Where fix_paths is defined as:
```python
def fix_paths(project_data, rel_path, extensions):
    """ Fix paths for extension list """
    norm_func = lambda path : os.path.normpath(os.path.join(rel_path, path))
    for key in extensions:
        if type(project_data[key]) is dict:
            for k,v in project_data[key].items():
                project_data[key][k] = [norm_func(i) for i in v]
        elif type(project_data[key]) is list:
            project_data[key] = [norm_func(i) for i in project_data[key]]
        else:
            project_data[key] = norm_func(project_data[key])
```

Is there a specific reason for this complexity? Why can't we just use the built in method relpath to make all files relative to the export directory. There should also be some functionality when paths are ALREADY relative to the export directory. It just seems crazy to MANUALLY append relative prefixes(..\\..\\....) to every path. Should relative paths be disallowed in the yaml/project dicts then?

issue_title: Generación incorrecta de `name` para datasets (y otras entidades)
issue_body:
**Contexto**

En CKAN varias entidades generan un `name` que actúa como "alias" y tiene caracter identificador (ese `name` apunta únicamente a una entidad y es reemplazable, generalmente, en su uso con el `identifier`).

Actualmente `pydatajson` está usando una función incorrecta para generar el `name` de un dataset:

http://datos.gob.ar/dataset/unidadesterritoriales

debería ser 

http://datos.gob.ar/dataset/unidades-territoriales

La transformación que se espera del nombre de un dataset reemplaza espacios con "-" guiones medios, no los elimina.

**Propuesta**

Existe ya una función en algún módulo de `pydatajson` llamada `title_to_name()`. Esta es la función que debemos usar *siempre* que este tipo de transformación sea requerida, porque nos asegura homogeneidad a lo largo de todas las partes donde se transformen strings de esta manera para generar `names`. 

Esta función debería estar OK, pero si está generando la eliminación de espacios en lugar de su reemplazo por "-", debería corregirse.

issue_title: How to wrap namespaces wit pybind11?

issue_body:
This has been spooking around in my head since at least a year. I have no idea how to solve it, but if nothing else, we need to document it.

## Introduction

The vdirsyncer storage API has two operations for writing events/contacts ("items"): `upload` and `update`. 

(The following is a simplified representation that ignores the differences between hrefs and UIDs, and asserts that hrefs are always directly derived from UIDs)

* `upload(item) -> etag`: If an item doesn't already exist with that UID, add `item` to the storage. Otherwise raise an error.
* `update(item, etag) -> new_etag`: If an item with the given `item`'s UID already exists and its etag is `etag`, replace it with `item`. Otherwise raise an error.

All of this is supposed to happen "atomically" (depending on the exact storage type):

* It must be impossible to call `upload(item)` rapidly at the same time with items that have the same UID, and get more than one success (=no errors).
* It must be impossible to call `update(item, etag)` rapidly at the same time with the same etag and get more than one success.

For the storage types `*dav`, `remotestorage`, `google_*`, we basically rely on the server to fullfill this guarantee.

## Problem

`filesystem` storage is tricker in that regard. Assuming that each item is called `<UID>.txt` in the folder `V`:

* `upload`:

  1. Create a temporary file in `V`, write the entire item to it.
  2. Call `link()` to create a second hardlink for the temporary file under the correct filename. This will fail if the target file already exists.
  3. To clean up (both in case of success or error), `unlink` the file from the temporary filename.

  Also the temporary file has the file extension `.tmp`, such that it is ignored when listing items, should it be left over for some reason (power loss).

  This implementation fullfills the above requirement for `upload`: It is impossible to `link` two files to the same filepath at the same time.

* `update`: Complete trashfire.

  1. Read `etag` from existing file. If the file doesn't exist, error. If the `etag` doesn't match, error.
  2. Create temporary file.
  3. Write to temporary file.
  4. `rename()` temporary filename to correct filename. This removes the previously existing file.

  Directly after Step 1, anything can happen. For example, two `update` operations from two clients could happen at the same time. Both clients read the etag, and see that it matches. Then they both overwrite the existing file. Both operations succeed, but only one item actually gets written. The other item is lost.

To be honest I doubt anything of this matters. As far as I can tell, no vdir-software other than vdirsyncer actually checks the etags before writing items.

Awaiting your comments. In hindsight this reads like rambling.

If use `--quiet` option, I get no output at all (as long as there are no errors).

If I do not use `--quiet` option, the output is fairly verbose:

```

## Desired behaviour

I'm running `certbot-auto renew` inside a cron job. And I would like to see output from `certbot-auto` in case of either an error or in case certbot actually renewed some certificate. And, in case no renewal is needed, certbot would not output anything (as is usual with `--quiet` flag).

issue_title: Use a proper gas value per transaction
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

The contract proxies are using the [Morden gaslimit](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/d79e8c32b7c26f4fd57ca3b6efa8686815b5c5a4/raiden/network/rpc/client.py#L34) for each transaction, this should be replaced by an [estimated gas](https://github.com/ethereum/wiki/wiki/JavaScript-API#web3ethestimategas).

### Why it happened

The current JSON-RPC implementation used by raiden is from `pyethapp.rpc_client`, this implementation uses [`eth_gaslimit`](https://github.com/ethereum/pyethapp/blob/afc8f66b8e47d6b21ea780e754c300a52b32411d/pyethapp/rpc_client.py#L412-L413) if the user does not specify the transaction gas, [`eth_gaslimit`](https://github.com/ethereum/pyethapp/blob/develop/pyethapp/jsonrpc.py#L1008-L1011) is a pyethapp extension that returns the latest block gas_limit, this can result in transactions failling because of improper gas setting.

As a workaround the Morden gaslimit was used.

## Solution

Use `eth_estimateGas`

## Tasklist

- [ ] Replace all occurrences of `startgas` in the `network.rpc.client` module with the estimated gas from the proxy `MethodProxy.estimate_gas`, e.g.:

From:
```python
        transaction_hash = self.proxy.approve.transact(
            contract_address,
            allowance,
            startgas=self.startgas,
            gasprice=self.gasprice,
        )
```
To:
```python
        estimated_gas = self.proxy.approve.estimate_gas(
            contract_address,
            allowance,
            startgas=estimated_gas,
            gasprice=self.gasprice,
        )
        transaction_hash = self.proxy.approve.transact(
            contract_address,
            allowance,
            startgas=estimated_gas,
            gasprice=self.gasprice,
        )
```

## Problem Definition

Users are reporting problems with our polling timeout that defaults to 10 seconds (#1064 #1058 and also on the gitter channel). This behavior is even more surprising because the polling fail, but the transaction did not, so the transaction will complete after a few more seconds and the second request from the use will fail again.

## Solution

My proposed solution is to remove *all timeouts* from the "middleware", pushing the timeouts to the edges, and only err when the transaction actually failed (either it was mined and the status is failure or it was removed from the transactions poll). This change would affect primarily the blockchain proxies that currently expose a synchronous interface.

issue_title: Use geth's clique PoA in tests/CI
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

The PoW based `geth` blockchain backend degraded in speed since we initially implemented it. Not only we are bitten by difficulty adjustment for long tests, but also does `geth` internally delay mining if we progress too fast:
```
INFO [07-24|15:38:44] Mining too far in the future             wait=2s
```

PoW furthermore requires DAG preparation (up to 10 minutes) and storage/caching (>=1GB), memory (>=1GB) and a considerable amount of CPU.

Since we run our tests with only miner node and do not test for consensus edge-cases (as of yet), we can switch to [clique PoA](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/issues/225), which is supported by `geth` and which we also use as backend for the `raiden smoketest` command.

This will minimize the use of resources and will allow for a rather stable block time of (minimally) around 1 second.

## Solution

I started the work on this in a branch at https://github.com/konradkonrad/raiden/tree/tests_with_clique -- initial results are quite promising: the `long_running` test set takes 200 seconds with PoA instead of 420 seconds with PoW on my machine.

What is missing from my branch is the proper encoding of the authorized miner/signer address (and only this one) in the genesis block ([see here](https://github.com/konradkonrad/raiden/commit/c1b69a9a0ef54fb19d1ae5a073c3be51bfa44883#diff-60a9b084203a3ca241410ac196d6065dR245) and [here](https://github.com/konradkonrad/raiden/commit/c1b69a9a0ef54fb19d1ae5a073c3be51bfa44883#diff-282099ef9fa82f2e3cbda8360e9c1689R139) ) for all fixture paths. 

The simplest approach to this could be to use a fixed miner account across all tests and probably remove all code that allows for multi-node test setups.
## Tasklist

- [ ] fix the signer address in fixture genesis blocks
- [ ] remove DAG generation and caching from `.travis.yml`
- [ ] assess test run time for all `geth` backed tests (especially for tests that only use a low number of blocks)

issue_title: Make `Secret` message usable as a balance proof
issue_body:
For a mediated transfer ABC these messages will be exchanged:

```
A>B MediatedTransfer
B>C MediatedTransfer
C>A SecretRequest
A>C RevealSecret
C>B RevealSecret
B>C Secret
B>A RevealSecret
A>B Secret
```
After these messages are exchanged, neither B nor C has an up-to-date message with the current `transferred_amount` that can be used to close/settle the channel (related to issue #181), either a new DirectTransfer or MediatedTransfer needs to be exchanged from A>B and B>C to provide a new balance proof.

Making the messages that do state changes (`DirectTransfer` and `Secret`) the messages used as balance_proof removes the need for an additional transfer among these channels.

- [ ] Add to the `Secret` message the fields required to use it in the smart contract (related to issue #43):
  - [ ] `transferred_amount`
  - [ ] `locksroot`
  - [ ] `nonce`
  - [ ] `recipient`
- [ ] Add decoding logic for the `Secret` message into the `NettingChannelLibrary`

issue_title: Decreasing number of running processes in non-task mode
issue_body: It will take some work to prepare a MWE, but just in case this is something obvious from the code I'm going to report my problem here for now. Running large number of small benchmark-style jobs with `concurrent = True` in non-task mode, I noticed that the workflow starts with multiple threads, then the number of concurrent threads drop as the workflow executes further, until at last it dropped to computing using only one thread towards the end. I recall we have implemented some file-based system to control for the number of concurrent jobs. Are there any switches or debug information somewhere that I can use to check if the CPU slots have been wrongfully reserved when there are in fact available slots?

issue_title: Shouldn't the settings inside a  Plugin specific configuration file get top priority above others?
issue_body:
Hello all and thanks for this great tool,

I use a small Batch script with this program and have some parameters set in stone like the quality "Best", usually I just have to enter the URL and select a very few options in my script and i'm good to go.

The problem is when i try to use a " Plugin specific configuration file", the settings i set there do not get the priority above either the initial command line or the " streamlinkrc " general configuration file and this isn't logic for me.

Let's say my script ends up in a command like : 
`Streamlink URL Best`

and that in the streamlinkrc file i have : 
`player="path to MPC-HC"`

But due to some specific streams being problematic or delivering too slow the streams i have  this on my _streamlinkrc.PluginName_  file : 
```
player="path to FFplay"
default-stream=480p
```

In those cases what i will get in the end is still the  "Best " quality played in MP-HC, not the 480p played in FFPlay, the settings from the Plugin specific configuration file _streamlinkrc.PluginName_ won't get the priority above others if they are already set before.

If i had set the command line with `Streamlink --player="path to FFplay" URL Best` i had the player i wanted as there it gets the priority but i still won't get the stream quality i set in the _streamlinkrc.pluginName_  file.

I think it would be logic that the settings from a Plugin specific configuration file should always have top priority and bypass any setting already set before if it exists, either set on the command line or in the streamlinkrc general settings file. 
If a setting is set but also exists inside the Plugin specific configuration file then the later should be the one used.

I'm not sure if this is possible to be done simply or not at all, or if maybe i am missing something and doing something wrong, but each time i tried to use a specific plugin config file it always ended up  in not getting priority if setting was already existing on the command line or in the the streamlinkrc file.

Thanks a lot for the hard work you guys put on this tool and sorry for bothering you with this :)

Would it perhaps make sense to print help instead?

With the current model for hooking third party checks into the permissions cog, we need permissions to be loaded first and not be reloaded. This was a design oversight on my part. 

I'll be postponing my other WIP stuff to resolve this in a satisfactory manner prior to b16, but this will require changes to how other checks which want to integrate into the permissions cog rather than operate separately need to function.

For anyone currently designing a 3rd party cog which uses these hooks: 
After the intended changes, any function which is an attribute of your cog should be possible to use as an integrated check. Each cog will get 1 before and 1 after check with expected names.

`__red_permissions_before` and `__red_permissions_after`
These can call your already existing functions if needed, and will receive the same arguments the current which current checks already do.

issue_title: Dataset add should not attempt to auto-match lineage datasets if they exist in db
issue_body:
When adding datasets to the database `datacube dataset add` does roughly following

- For every input dataset
   1. Auto-match dataset to a product (fail if not found)
   2. For every dataset in the lineage of an input dataset
         1. Auto-match dataset to the product (fail if not found)

If you are indexing derived dataset with lineage that already exists in the database, then second auto-match is not necessary, those datasets are already in the database, and are already associated to a product. 

This behaviour makes `--dtype` option behave in a way that most users won't expect, when indexing datasets with lineage. Here is a help string for this option:

```
  -t, --dtype TEXT                Product to be associated with the datasets
```

Several problems already

1. Using `dtype` name that is highly associated with `numpy.dtype` in the target community, but is not *that kind of dtype*
2. Actually means `dataset-type` == `product`
3. Inconsistent naming on one line: *"dataset type"* on a command line, *"product"* in the help string
4. You can actually specify multiple products, help doesn't make this clear

What this option really is: 

> Restrain auto-matching to a subset of products from the database (you can supply several on a command line), auto-matching is applied to the new datasets listed on the command line and any lineage datasets referenced in the metadata.

### User experience scenario

1. Created some new datasets using stats tool or similar (so they have lineage data)
2. Added product definition for my new datasets
3. Tried running with default options and was told that my datasets could match to several products in the DB and so no indexing can proceed
   - `datacube product add` will not warn you when adding products with "too similar" metadata
4. No worries I can specify product with this weirdly named `--dtype` option
5. Try running `datacube dataset add -t my_new_product my_new_dataset.nc`, still get an error

But I just told you which product to use!!?

The problem is that now that you have restrained set of products to match against to just one product you care about, you can't auto-match any of the lineage products at all. 

But you should not need to, those datasets are already in the DB, no need to auto-match them in this case. Sure you still need to auto-match IF and ONLY IF lineage datasets are not in the DB already.

### Conclusion

Combination of poor documentation and decision to auto-match before checking DB makes `--dtype` option unusable to anyone but those who are ultimately familiar with the implementation of `datacube dataset add` command.

issue_title: It takes some time to make a message invisible after receiving it
issue_body:
When you receive a message, it takes 2~5 seconds for it to be invisible, so, if you have multiple consumers, all of them will receive the same message.
Besides, if you're receiving a message from a Queue that is subscribed to a Topic with RawMessageDelivery set as true, the second time you get the message, the body will have the whole envelop and not just the body message.

Just an example:

The request:
http://localhost:4576/queue/my_queue?Action=ReceiveMessage

The first request

```xml
<ReceiveMessageResponse
	xmlns="http://queue.amazonaws.com/doc/2012-11-05/">
	<ReceiveMessageResult>
		<Message>
			<MessageId>b7ed434f-a569-47d4-999d-acaa4c56d6f6</MessageId>
			<ReceiptHandle>b7ed434f-a569-47d4-999d-acaa4c56d6f6#1e1f9b64-b9b2-4629-8799-3ae51d29068a</ReceiptHandle>
			<MD5OfBody>afbaac44c20a6c66edb2e7c5f6f34bb8</MD5OfBody>
			<Body>{ "id": 1 }</Body>
		</Message>
	</ReceiveMessageResult>
	<ResponseMetadata>
		<RequestId>00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000</RequestId>
	</ResponseMetadata>
</ReceiveMessageResponse>
```
The second request

```xml
<ReceiveMessageResponse
	xmlns="http://queue.amazonaws.com/doc/2012-11-05/">
	<ReceiveMessageResult>
		<Message>
			<MessageId>b7ed434f-a569-47d4-999d-acaa4c56d6f6</MessageId>
			<ReceiptHandle>b7ed434f-a569-47d4-999d-acaa4c56d6f6#1e1f9b64-b9b2-4629-8799-3ae51d29068a</ReceiptHandle>
			<MD5OfBody>afbaac44c20a6c66edb2e7c5f6f34bb8</MD5OfBody>
			<Body>{"Message": "{ \"id\": 1 }", "Type": "Notification", "TopicArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:my_topic", "MessageId": "1c4cae5d-51c1-42a8-9b86-d636f5564bc4"}</Body>
		</Message>
	</ReceiveMessageResult>
	<ResponseMetadata>
		<RequestId>00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000</RequestId>
	</ResponseMetadata>
</ReceiveMessageResponse>l
```

Does anyone know what could be happening?

issue_title: How to search Sentinel-2 L2A imagery
issue_body:
Hi,
I am using the sentinelhub.aws command-line tool to download an atmospherically-corrected Sentinel-2 scene for my tile of interest. After configuring my AWS credentials, I can use the command `sentinelhub.aws --tile T33UWR 2018-05-01 -e -f $HOME/downloaded_images --l2a` to download the L2A product in .SAFE format. It works perfectly.

Now I'm looking for a method to search which dates have Sentinel-2 L2A data available on AWS for my tile of interest.

Right now I am running a command such as `sentinelhub.aws --tile T33UWR {date} -i --l2a` for each date in my time-period of interest and checking if the result throws an exception, but I'm sure there must be a better method of doing a search.

Is there a method or command line option for searching available S2 L2A imagery? If there is, it would be great if you can add an L2A search example in the documentation.

Thanks,
Jiri Kadlec
www.gisat.cz

issue_title: prophyle-classify: Add a parameter for propagating assignments to the leaf level
issue_body: The RASE code can be reused for that.

I'd love to see an asynchronous version of DDPG on here. Would anyone be able to help me with it?

Here are quick thoughts:

A3C seems to be king of the hill at the moment, but DDPG has some clear advantages in terms of generalizing better, and ultimately is very similar in its implementation to A2C.  It just doesn't multithread, so A3C can win with greater musclepower. But if DDPG had a readily available and easy to use async version as well in Keras-RL, I think it would get a lot of widespread love.

However, the A3C implementation in the experimental branch never quite got done, so it's going to take some effort to get that up and running, then rework it into an Async DDPG. I think I know the problem though.
In the [paper,](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1602.01783.pdf) Deepmind states that all the actor / critic layers should be shared (i.e. with common weights) except the last one, but in this A3C implementation, the actors and critics seem to have independent networks. I suspect that's the reason there are issue threads here regarding its performance. The shared weights seem to be a fundamental mechanism by which these asynchronous threads work together toward the common goal, so with the actors and critics being independent networks, it seems like memory recall with a common pool of memories would be the only thing binding them together. I could be wrong though. 

Next, one of the main differences between A3C and an async DDPG is the fact that DDPG takes action as an additional input with state in the critic network.  However, I'm not entirely sure if you also need to share the core layers of the actor and critic networks in an async DDPG implementation the way you're supposed to with A3C, and if you are, how you would do that. I saw one implementation (which I'll have to find again) where the output of the critic was simply multiplied by the action input right before the last layer, but that doesn't totally make sense to me. In that implementation though the actors and critics did share weights up till that point. I do see how maybe you could have two neural networks (one built just on state, and a simpler one on action with maybe just one layer) that merge together, and then have maybe 1-2 additional hidden layers before the output. I'm not sure if that would totally work, if there's a better way to do it, or if that's just totally not necessary.

I did find another A3C implementation built with Keras that has worked well for me. [Check this out.](https://github.com/rlcode/reinforcement-learning/blob/master/2-cartpole/5-a3c/cartpole_a3c.py). You can see that the actor / critic layers are shared, though they did diverge for more than just the last layer. In my version, I kept it like in the paper and all layers were shared except the output (critic just has the Q value as output, and actor gets a softmax vector). For async DDPG, I think it would be a similar network setup, just with a more complex critic network that also works the action in somewhere. I just don't know how many layers should be shared with the actor(s).
